title: RepoBrief CodeGraph selective adoption
goal: Adopt the useful structural-navigation ideas from CodeGraph as a deterministic read-only Lenskit surface without introducing a second index authority, memory store or merge gate.
completion: Lenskit exposes a bounded agent impact/edit context built from existing graph, symbol, query and provenance artifacts.
Related tests, incoming/outgoing relations, contracts, docs and entrypoints are separated by evidence type and source status.
A fixed evaluation establishes measurable navigation benefit or the candidate remains opt-in or is removed.
RepoBrief remains read-only and produces no review verdict, risk score, coverage guarantee or merge authorization.

metadata.source_request: alles umsetzen (vorher natürlich im bureau registrieren)
metadata.boundary_decision: selective Lenskit-native adoption; no persistent CodeGraph service or memory layer
metadata.outcome: Useful CodeGraph-style structural navigation was adopted natively in Lenskit, measured on coherent live bundles, repaired after a real regression, and retained as an opt-in read-only surface. CodeGraph itself was not installed and no second index, memory store or authority channel was created.
metadata.separate_future_decision: Any default-route promotion requires a broader agent benchmark and a new explicitly registered task.
